title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss joined the Patriots in 2007 for a fourth-round pick, setting an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in his first season.
Wes Welker was acquired from the Dolphins in 2007, becoming a prolific slot receiver and complementing Randy Moss effectively.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, recording over 700 yards before his suspension ended his season early.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning for arguing a check swing strike call against Trevor Story.
Trevor Story and Alex Cora expressed frustration over umpire C.B. Bucknor's decision not to appeal to the first base umpire.
Cora emphasized the importance of using the game as a learning opportunity for better ABS strategy in future games.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor for a controversial strike three call during the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected after arguing the call, which Bucknor did not double-check with the first base umpire.
The Red Sox tied the game later with Wilyer Abreu's ninth-inning homer but ultimately lost in the 11th inning.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals for the Bruins since returning from the Olympic break.
Zacha missed the Olympics due to a concussion but focused on improving his NHL performance.
Zacha's career-high goal tally is 26, making him a key player in Boston's second line.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray struggled in his Red Sox debut, allowing four runs in four innings against the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray's improvement after the first inning but highlighted the need for better defense.
The Red Sox bullpen performed well after Gray's exit, with newcomers posting scoreless frames before the 11th inning loss.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands rallied at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' protest, part of nationwide demonstrations.
The protest featured performances by the Dropkick Murphys and speeches from leaders like Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren.
Over 160 rallies were held across Massachusetts, making it the state with the second-highest number of protests on Saturday.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 17-song set at the House of Blues in Boston, lasting 90 minutes and featuring theatrical shock rock elements.
The band used fake blood and elaborate costumes to engage the audience, covering songs like "Pink Pony Club" by Chappell Roan.
GWAR's set featured political and social satire, targeting figures like Donald Trump and Kristi Noem in a graphic stage performance.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are questionable for the Celtics' game against the Charlotte Hornets due to Achilles issues.
Neemias Queta and Derrick White are uncertain to play, while Nikola Vucevic is out with a fracture for the Celtics.
Jayson Tatum expressed frustration with personal performance but continues contributing, logging 12 rebounds against the Hawks.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the nationwide 'No Kings' protest, with over 160 rallies across Massachusetts.
Speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Ed Markey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and Rep. Ayanna Pressley. Performances featured the Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters expressed concerns about democracy, global tensions, and opposition to centralized power, with some in costumes for impact.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, 2026, part of a statewide movement.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd during the Boston rally.
Over 160 'No Kings' events took place across Massachusetts, with Boston serving as a central protest location.
